sections.heading: In-Canada applicants
items: We’ve extended the temporary public policy to exempt certain foreign nationals in Canada from the immigration medical examination requirement until October 5, 2029 . Updated
If you completed an IME within 5 years of submitting your new application, include the IME number (or the unique medical identifier number) from your previous IME in your current application.

sections.heading: Who needs a medical exam
Changes to countries and territories where an immigration medical exam is required
items: As of November 3, 2025, updates were made to the list of countries and territories where an immigration medical exam (IME) is required . Check the updated list before getting your IME to confirm if you need one.
This update does not affect applications submitted before November 3, 2025.

sections.heading: If you plan to stay for 6 months or less
items: You generally don’t need an immigration medical exam unless you plan to work in certain jobs in which public health must be protected.
Examples of such jobs are
bullets: jobs that bring you into close contact with people, such as workers in health care settings clinical laboratory workers patient attendants in nursing and geriatric homes medical students admitted to Canada to attend university medical electives and physicians on short-term locums workers in primary or secondary school settings, or workers in child-care settings domestics workers who give in-home care to children, the elderly and the disabled day nursery employees other similar jobs
patient attendants in nursing and geriatric homes
medical students admitted to Canada to attend university
medical electives and physicians on short-term locums
workers in primary or secondary school settings, or workers in child-care settings
workers who give in-home care to children, the elderly and the disabled
agricultural workers who’ve visited or lived in one of these countries for 6 months or more in a row in the year before they came to Canada

sections.heading: If you plan to stay for more than 6 months
items: You need a medical exam if at least one of these applies to you :
If you need an immigration medical exam, the visa office will tell you what to do next.
bullets: You have lived in or travelled to one or more of these countries or territories for 6 months or more in a row in the year before you come to Canada.
You’ll come to Canada to work in a job in which public health must be protected .
You’re applying for a parent and grandparent super visa .

sections.heading: Types of medical exams
items: There are 2 types of immigration medical exams:
You cannot request a specific type of medical exam. We’ll let you know which exam you need.

sections.heading: Who can do your exam
When to get your medical exam
items: You can either get your medical exam before or after you apply.

sections.heading: Before you submit your application
items: You have the option of getting an exam before you apply. This is called an upfront medical exam.
You can get one if you apply to
To get one, contact a panel physician directly. If you present yourself for an upfront medical exam, the panel physician will conduct a standard medical exam.
bullets: visit (including parent and grandparent super visa )

sections.heading: After you submit your application
items: We’ll send you instructions on how to get your medical exam done. Please read these instructions to find out
If you don’t follow these instructions, we may refuse your application.
bullets: the type of medical exam you need to get

sections.heading: What to bring
items: When you go to your appointment for the medical exam, you must bring
If you’re referred for an X-ray or other tests, you may be asked to present your identification again when you go for those tests.
bullets: proper identification We strongly recommend you bring your passport as your identification document. You can also use other approved identification documents, including a national identity (ID) card a driver’s license issued from one the following countries (if you’re still in that country) Australia Canada New Zealand United Kingdom United States an original birth certificate (if the child is under 18 years old) a refugee travel document a Red Cross travel document a UN laissez-passer a seaman’s book an Organization of American States travel document a refugee protection claimant document

an Organization of American States travel document
eye glasses or contact lenses, if you wear them
any medical reports or test results that you have for any previous or existing medical conditions
a list of your current medications
the Medical Report form (IMM 1017E or IMM1020E), if you don’t get an up front medical exam We’ll send you this form
4 recent photographs , if the panel physician doesn’t use eMedical Contact the panel physician before your appointment to find out.

sections.heading: If you were previously vaccinated
items: We encourage you to bring any proof of previous vaccinations , including vaccination against
The proof of vaccination is not mandatory . If you give consent, the panel physician will

sections.heading: What you need to pay for
items: You must pay all fees related to the medical exam when you’re there, including
If you choose to get any of the vaccines the panel physician offers you, you may have to pay for them. This depends on the panel physician’s fees and local jurisdiction practices.
If we refuse your application after your medical exam, we won’t refund those fees.
bullets: the fee for the doctor or radiologist
any special tests, investigations or treatment needed
any specialists you need to see

sections.heading: What to do if you can’t complete your medical exam
items: You or your family member may not be able to complete a medical exam:
If this is the case, please submit a letter of explanation showing why you can’t meet this requirement and we’ll review your request.
You can contact us:
bullets: because you can’t access a panel physician in your region
using the web form (opens in a new tab) select “Add a document to your application”
select “Add a document to your application”

sections.heading: What to expect during your exam
If you’re getting a standard medical exam
When you arrive
items: The panel physician or clinic staff will ask you for identification to confirm your identity. If you’re referred for an x-ray or other tests, you may be asked to present your identification again when you go for those tests. Your picture will also be taken for our records.

sections.heading: Medical history questionnaire
items: The doctor will fill out a medical history questionnaire with you. This questionnaire is about any previous or existing medical conditions. They’ll also ask you about any medications you’re taking.

sections.heading: Physical examination
items: You’ll undergo a physical exam.
The doctor or medical clinic staff will:
bullets: listen to your heart and lungs

sections.heading: Other possible tests
items: Depending on your age, you may be asked to do chest x-rays and laboratory tests at the clinic or a laboratory. This is routine screening and the doctor will discuss any abnormal results with you.

You may be referred to a specialist for more testing, depending on the results of your medical exam. Complete this request as soon as possible to avoid delays in the processing of your medical examination.
You should bring any proof of previous vaccination with you to your exam. If there are vaccines available, the panel physician may offer you vaccinations for preventable diseases.
If you give consent, the panel physician will
If you consent to receive a vaccine and you consent to share this information with us, the panel physician will record and share
Keep all paper copies the panel physician gives you for your personal records.
Get more information about vaccines and immunization
The panel physician may offer you a Government of Canada-accepted COVID-19 vaccine if it is available. Vaccination is completely voluntary and is not required as part of your exam.
If you receive the COVID-19 vaccine during your exam, the panel physician will record it.
If you’ve already been vaccinated for COVID-19, even if you have only received 1 dose,
Get more information on travel and COVID-19 vaccines:
bullets: record any proof of vaccine you bring with you
the reason a vaccination wasn’t offered or administered
bring proof of your vaccination to your exam Proof can be a paper copy or an electronic version
Proof can be a paper copy or an electronic version
the panel physician will record your proof of vaccination with your exam
keep a copy of the vaccination record given to you by the panel physician
COVID-19: Travel, testing, quarantine and borders

sections.heading: Right to have a chaperone
items: You have the right to a chaperone at any time during the medical exam.
You may:
If you have questions or feel uncomfortable with a part of the exam, please ask the panel physician to stop and tell them about your concerns.
bullets: ask the medical clinic to have a staff member in the room
stop the exam at any time to ask questions about what the doctor is doing
stop the exam and ask for a chaperone, even if you refused one at first

sections.heading: After your exam is done
items: Once the exam is done, the physician will send us the results.
The physician will give you a document confirming that you had a medical exam. Keep the document with you as proof of your immigration medical exam.
If you’re unsatisfied with how the panel physician or panel radiologist did your medical exam, you may
We welcome all compliments, comments or observations through our feedback form .
bullets: complain using our Web form or

sections.heading: Getting a copy of your medical exam results
items: If you want a copy of your medical exam results, please ask the physician when you’re there.
Medical reports and X-rays that are part of the medical exam become our property. We cannot return them to you.

sections.heading: How to include your medical exam results with your application
If you had an upfront medical exam
items: When you have an upfront medical exam, the physician will ask you to fill out an IMM 1017B Upfront Medical Report. You must include a copy of the report with your paper application. If you apply online, you must upload the report before you can submit your application.

sections.heading: If you had a streamlined medical exam or you had your medical exam after submitting your application
items: You don’t need to send us anything.

sections.heading: How long your medical results are good for
items: Your medical exam results are good for 12 months only. If you’re a student or worker, your letter of introduction shows the date your results expire (if you received it after November 30, 2021).
If you don’t come to Canada as a visitor, student or worker within that time, you may need to do another exam.

sections.heading: If you’re getting a streamlined medical exam

items: When you have an upfront medical exam, the physician will ask you to fill out an IMM 1017B Upfront Medical Report. You must include a copy of the report with your paper application. If you apply online, you must upload the 1017B Upfront Medical Report before you can submit your application.

items: Your medical exam results are valid for 12 months only. If you’re a student or worker, your letter of introduction shows the date your results expire (if you received it after November 30, 2021).
If you don’t come to Canada as a visitor, student or worker within that time, you may need to do another exam.
